commit.message:
Fix Zed dev extension grammar pin

**Major Changes:**
1. Replace the stale unreachable pinned grammar revision in the Zed extension manifest and manifest renderer.
2. Add a Zed CI regression check that fails when the checked-in pinned grammar revision is not reachable from the current repo history.

**Why:**
- Fresh Zed dev extension installs use the checked-in portable manifest, and the previous pinned grammar rev no longer existed in reachable repository history.
- That made Zed fail while installing the dev extension because it could not build the pinned grammar source cleanly from a valid reachable revision.

**Testing:**
- ./test/ci/editor-zed.sh

**Changes:**
- Updated the portable pinned grammar SHA in the checked-in extension manifest and renderer.
- Added a focused editor-zed guard so stale unreachable grammar pins are caught before release.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Implement module system (#9)

* Commit plan

* Add module headers and discovery graph groundwork

**Detailed explanation:**
- add `import`, `export`, and `as` to the syntax surface, AST, lowering pipeline, and parser ordering rules so module headers are represented explicitly instead of being hidden in ad hoc text parsing
- add a dedicated `frontend` library with module-graph types plus recursive discovery that resolves module-vs-member imports, detects ambiguity, topologically orders dependencies, and allocates per-file parser ID ranges
- keep the current single-file typecheck helpers building by treating header declarations as metadata-only nodes in existing syntax and typecheck traversals until import resolution/per-module checking lands

**Testing:**
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/syntax --force`
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force`

**Changes:**
- `docs/plans/todo/language/02_module-system.md`: add and update the `PROGRESS` log for the implemented M1/M2 groundwork
- `lib/frontend/syntax/*`: add module-header tokens, AST nodes, parsing, lowering, and parser/lexer tests
- `lib/frontend/module_context.ml` and `lib/frontend/discovery.ml`: add module graph types, cycle detection, ambiguity detection, discovery, and ID-offset tests
- `lib/frontend/typecheck/*`: treat import/export declarations as metadata-only during existing helper traversals

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Implement module compilation pipeline

**Major Changes:**
1. Add a frontend project compiler that discovers reachable modules, resolves semantic imports, rewrites module-visible names to stable internal identifiers, extracts typed module signatures, and compiles modules in dependency order.
2. Wire the CLI build/run/check commands through the entry-file compiler while preserving the legacy single-file fast path for programs without module headers.
3. Expose the new frontend compiler through library boundaries and teach the Go emitter and LSP statement walkers to treat import/export declarations as compile-time-only top-level forms.

**Why:**
- The language plan requires per-module checking, direct-import visibility rules, ambiguity detection, and one flattened Go package across the reachable module graph.
- The previous pipeline only handled single-file programs and could not validate or emit module-based builds.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules modules_edge modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots

**Changes:**
- Added project compiler/import resolver/signature extraction modules.
- Threaded prepare_state through inference/checker entry points for seeded per-module runs.
- Updated CLI diagnostics lookup for multi-file errors.
- Added import/export exhaustiveness handling in emitter and LSP helpers.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Add module fixtures and snapshot coverage

**Major Changes:**
1. Add a curated multi-module fixture corpus covering namespace imports, direct imports, nested module aliases, imported types/traits/shapes, impl visibility, ambiguity errors, private export rejection, local shadowing, transitive visibility failure, private-name isolation, and Go-keyword collisions.
2. Teach the integration harness to treat  groups as entrypoint suites by running  fixtures only, so support modules are not executed as standalone programs.
3. Add exact emitted-Go snapshots for module import mangling, trait-plus-inherent qualification, and Go-keyword collision cases, and update the module-system plan progress log with the completed implementation and verification notes.

**Why:**
- Module behavior needs regression coverage for both runtime semantics and emitted Go naming/resolution, especially now that imports, visibility, and qualification interact across files.
- Directory-based module fixtures require entrypoint-aware harness behavior to reflect the locked project-root decision.

**Testing:**
- ./test/integration.sh modules modules_edge modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force

**Changes:**
- Added module fixture directories and normalized them to the entry-directory project root rule.
- Added three generated-Go snapshot files plus snapshot-suite coverage.
- Recorded implementation status, findings, and focused verification in the plan doc.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Reject conflicting module import bindings

**Major Changes:**
1. Detect direct-import and namespace-import name collisions instead of silently overwriting earlier bindings during module rewrite.
2. Preserve builtin/direct value precedence when resolving dotted expressions so module namespaces do not steal normal value lookups.
3. Add edge fixtures for conflicting imports and builtin-vs-namespace precedence, plus a resolver unit test for stable qualified-name segment ordering.

**Why:**
- Last-write-wins import binding behavior hid real conflicts and made module resolution order-dependent.
- Qualified namespace lookup must defer to real value bindings at the root segment to preserve normal language semantics.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_edge

**Changes:**
- Added import collision diagnostics in the module resolver.
- Tightened qualified-name rewriting to respect builtin/direct/current-module values before namespace traversal.
- Added focused modules_edge coverage for these failure modes.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Validate trait impl coherence across module graphs

**Major Changes:**
1. Rewrite reachable modules once up front and reuse those rewrites during project compilation.
2. Pre-register trait impl heads across the whole reachable module graph so duplicate impls are rejected even when they arrive through separate imports.
3. Add focused compiler tests and a multi-file modules_edge fixture for transitive duplicate-impl rejection.

**Why:**
- Per-module checking previously missed conflicting impls that only became visible after flattening the full module graph.
- Module builds need a project-wide coherence pass before typechecking modules independently.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_edge

**Changes:**
- Added a build-wide rewrite cache and impl registration pass in the frontend compiler.
- Threaded cached module rewrites into per-module compilation.
- Added regression coverage for transitive duplicate impl visibility.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Stabilize module mangling snapshot coverage

**Major Changes:**
1. Escape module-path components and exported member names when generating internal identifiers so dots, underscores, and sigils stay injective.
2. Add module codegen fixtures for namespace-qualified exported values, nested type qualification, colliding module prefixes, and sigil-name escape collisions.
3. Refresh exact Go snapshots and snapshot-suite coverage to lock the emitted naming and qualification behavior.

**Why:**
- Flattened module builds need internal names that remain distinct across path-shape collisions and escaped user identifiers.
- The new module surface area needs generated-Go regression coverage for both normal namespace qualification and aggressive naming edge cases.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots

**Changes:**
- Added escaping for internal module and member identifier components in the import resolver.
- Added three multi-file fixture groups that probe namespace value lowering and mangling collisions.
- Updated exact emitted-Go snapshots and registered them in the snapshot integration suite.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Format module-system OCaml changes

**Major Changes:**
1. Apply the repository formatter to branch-touched OCaml files in the module-system implementation.
2. Keep the branch aligned with the mandatory quality gate before opening the PR.

**Why:**

**Testing:**

**Changes:**

commit.message:
Fix 8 bugs found by automated swarm analysis (#194)

* Add failing tests for 8 bugs found by automated swarm analysis

Tests cover:
- SINTERSTORE dest incorrectly marked as read+write (should be write-only)
- MIGRATE KEYS clause fails with 2+ keys (kw startfrom=-2 too narrow)
- Django async wrapper missing lock_timeout forwarding to explore_async_dpor
- SQLAlchemy async wrapper missing lock_timeout forwarding to explore_async_dpor
- Django async wrapper cursor leak (no try/finally around cursor)
- SQLAlchemy sync wrapper __exit__(None, None, None) ignores exception info
- CooperativeRLock.acquire() missing _in_dpor_machinery() guard
- explore_marker_interleavings except (TimeoutError, Exception) too broad

All tests currently fail, demonstrating the bugs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TecRomc3fA3qKiNkrCGcgV

* Fix 8 bugs found by automated swarm analysis

1. SINTERSTORE dest marked read+write -> write-only (like SDIFFSTORE)
2. MIGRATE KEYS clause kw startfrom=-2 -> 5 (finds KEYS with 2+ keys)
3. Django async: forward lock_timeout to explore_async_dpor
4. SQLAlchemy async: forward lock_timeout to explore_async_dpor
5. Django async: use context manager for cursor (prevents leak on error)
6. SQLAlchemy sync: pass exc_info to __exit__ (enables proper rollback)
7. CooperativeRLock.acquire: add _in_dpor_machinery() guard (like Lock)
8. explore_marker_interleavings: catch only TimeoutError, not Exception

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TecRomc3fA3qKiNkrCGcgV

commit.message:
Pre-modules parity and hardening (#8)

* Update plans

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Support current pattern-match codegen parity

**Detailed explanation:**
- remove the remaining backend rejections for multi-pattern arms, nested constructor payload patterns, nested record field patterns, and record patterns inside constructor payloads
- validate that or-pattern alternatives bind the same names and resolve bindings across every alternative instead of only the first pattern
- add focused fixture coverage for the previously unsupported lowering paths and lock the enum case-control-flow fix that avoids false fallback panics after successful nested payload matches

**Testing:**
- make unit compiler
- ./test/integration.sh test/fixtures/unions/u81_or_pattern_in_match_arm.mr test/fixtures/enums/e56_multi_pattern_arm_codegen_limitation.mr test/fixtures/codegen/cg11_specialized_body_codegen_error_is_surfaced_cleanly.mr test/fixtures/unions/u92_nested_constructor_payload_pattern_lowers.mr test/fixtures/unions/u93_record_pattern_inside_constructor_payload_lowers.mr test/fixtures/unions/u94_recursive_nested_or_pattern_lowers.mr test/fixtures/records/r187_nested_record_pattern_match_lowers.mr

**Changes:**

commit.message:
[gpu] Fix contour breaks and bounds quantization in encode (#5892)

Encode used adjacent endpoint equality to infer contour breaks, which
fails when two contours touch at the same point. Track contour starts
explicitly during accumulation instead, and use that flag when packing
shared endpoints.

Encode also rounded blob bounds to the nearest quarter unit. That can
shrink the encoded extents and clip coverage at glyph edges. Quantize
mins downward and maxes upward so the blob header always encloses the
actual geometry.

Add focused API tests for both regressions.

Testing:
- meson test -C build

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Raise max_tokens budgets, add size analysis to run summaries, and guard against output bloat

- Add sizes section to RunSummary (schema v2): min/max/avg stats for prompt history, user prompt, response content, thought, and utterance lengths using O(1) streaming accumulators

- Add MAX_THOUGHT_CHARS (200) cap in updateThought: prevents unbounded prompt growth from verbose thoughts injected into subsequent directives
- Propagate truncated_by_max_length flag through log pipeline and run summary filtering stats
